channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New hospital in Portage la Prairie delayed until 2027
item.description: The opening of the new hospital in Portage la Prairie, Manitoba, has been delayed and will not take place until next year, 2027. Originally scheduled to begin operations this year, the delay is due to...

item.title: Governor Lusaka Reorganises Bungoma County Executive to Improve Service Delivery
item.description: Bungoma County has experienced a major reshuffle in its executive team after Governor Kenneth Lusaka relieved all senior officials of their duties. The reorganisation affects every Member of the Count...

item.title: Holy Sites in Jerusalem Reopen After 41-Day Closure Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The reopening of Jerusalem's holy sites after 41 days of closure, caused by the ongoing conflict, was met with overwhelming emotions from worshippers of all three Abrahamic religions. Muslim worshippe...

item.title: PhilHealth Board Reshuffle Raises Questions on Transparency and Sectoral Representation
item.description: During the Holy Week of 2026, the PhilHealth Board underwent a quiet reshuffle that has drawn criticism from various sectors. The traditional sectoral representatives, who represented labor and employ...

item.title: Russia allows import of Pakistani potatoes after one-year ban
item.description: Russia has officially lifted the ban on importing potatoes from Pakistan, specifically from Punjab, effective April 8, 2026. The ban, imposed in May 2025, had been due to concerns over the alleged pre...

item.title: Assam Board 10th Result 2026: Check Dates, Websites, and Key Details
item.description: The Assam Higher Secondary Education Council (AHSEC) is set to announce the Assam Board 10th Result for 2026 on April 10th. Students who appeared for the examination can access their results through t...

item.title: Police Investigate Assault on Vihiga Senator in Kisumu
item.description: In Kisumu City, a group of hooded assailants violently attacked Vihiga Senator Godfrey Osotsi at a restaurant, leaving him seriously injured. The Senator, a deputy leader of ODM and part of a faction ...

item.title: Telecom infrastructure under heavy vandalism as theft cripple networks in 14 Nigerian states
item.description: For 2025 and early part of 2026, criminal groups don turn Nigeria’s telecom towers to open market for stealing things wey serious for communication. According to data from the Nigerian Communications ...

item.title: Kevin Quiambao named KBL Rookie of the Year
item.description: Filipino basketball player Kevin Quiambao has earned the title of Rookie of the Year in the Korean Basketball League (KBL) after a stellar first season. Quiambao, who plays for the Goyang Sono Skygunn...

item.title: Georgia youth softball coach arrested after attacking rival coach during game
item.description: A youth softball match in Georgia spiralled out of control when Meredith Haskew Grant, coach of an 8U little league team, assaulted the opposing team's coach. The incident occurred after Grant claimed...

item.title: Melania Trump denies Epstein links and calls for survivors’ hearing as survivors demand full release of files
item.description: First Lady Melania Trump has delivered a rare televised address from the White House in which she emphatically denied any friendship with or knowledge of the crimes of convicted sex offender Jeffrey E...

item.title: Adelabu’s 6,000MW Power Target Delayed Again, Nigeria Struggles with Persistent Blackouts
item.description: Adebayo Adelabu, Nigeria’s Minister of Power, made bold promises to boost the country’s electricity generation to 6,000MW by the end of 2023. However, as of 2026, Nigeria is still struggling to meet t...

item.title: Kisii Man Builds House for Stepfather, Siblings Gift Father Mercedes-Benz as Thanks
item.description: In a heartwarming show of gratitude, a Kisii man built a house for his stepfather, who had raised him as his own, defying the common notion that step-parents are often unkind to their stepchildren. Af...

item.title: Malema Confirms Friendship with Businessman Nxumalo, Denies Any Criminal Connections
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema has publicly confirmed his acquaintance with controversial businessman Zesimdumise “Ze” Nxumalo, while firmly denying any personal involvement in criminal activities. The stat...

item.title: London Mayor urges government to curb social media disinformation and strengthen regulation
item.description: Sadiq Khan, the Mayor of London, has urged ministers to take stronger action against social media platforms that enable the spread of disinformation and divisive narratives. Speaking at a disinformati...

item.title: Parliamentarians’ projects see faster spending than overall development budget
item.description: In the first nine months of the current fiscal year, Pakistan's government has spent less than half of its Public Sector Development Programme (PSDP) budget, with only 41.5 percent of the Rs1 trillion...

item.title: Mark Carney attends Canadiens game and meets players in Montreal
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney visited the Montreal Canadiens’ dressing room following their 2-1 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning on Thursday night. Carney, attending a Liberal party convention in Mon...

item.title: Australian shares slip as geopolitical uncertainty and tech sell-off weigh on markets
item.description: Australian stocks traded lower on Friday, with the S&P/ASX 200 down 0.4 per cent to 8935.6 as investors reacted to ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The Strait of Hormuz remained closed amid the Ir...

item.title: Gloria Arroyo refutes claims of attempt to unseat Speaker Faustino Dy III
item.description: Former President and Pampanga Second District Representative Gloria Macapagal Arroyo has denied accusations that she was behind efforts to remove Speaker Faustino “Bojie” Dy III from his position as t...

item.title: Apollo 16 Astronaut Charlie Duke Discusses Risks of Orion Spacecraft Reentry During Artemis II Mission
item.description: In an interview with CNN's Erin Burnett on OutFront, Charlie Duke, the youngest of the 12 astronauts who walked on the Moon during the Apollo program, shared insights into the intense experience await...
